name: research-rag
whenToUse: Answer a knowledge question with cited evidence from the corpus and/or the web; required when faithfulness and source attribution matter.
workflow: Decompose the question into sub-claims that each need a source.
Retrieve top-k local corpus chunks and (opt-in) web evidence.
Rerank by relevance; drop low-signal chunks instead of stuffing context.
Compose a compact, cited context — quote the exact supporting span per claim.
Answer only what the evidence supports; flag uncertainty and contested attributions.
Run the epistemic gate; ensure every claim maps to a source label.
verification: Every non-trivial claim has a source label (citation correctness).
No claim contradicts a doNotAttributeTo / disputed record.
Uncertainty is stated where evidence is weak or legendary.
commonFailures: Citing a source that does not actually support the claim.
Merging traditions/lineages the evidence keeps distinct.
Stating legendary/compiled attributions as settled fact.

examples.input: Did Confucius write the Dao De Jing?
examples.output: No. Source: data/attributions.json#dao_de_jing (doNotAttributeTo: confucius); attributed to the Laozi/Daoist tradition. Confidence: compiled/legendary — flag uncertainty.
